conversion_notes: ABSTRACTION-LEVEL MIXING: This is a 2-level decomposition flattened into a single level. PlaceOrder is both a child of OrderSystem AND the orchestrator of ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, ReserveInventory, CreateOrderRecord. CancelOrder is both a child AND orchestrator of VerifyOrderNotShipped, RefundPayment, RestoreInventory, UpdateOrderStatus. PlaceOrder.behavior: 'Call ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, ReserveInventory, CreateOrderRecord in sequence'. CancelOrder.behavior: 'Call VerifyOrderNotShipped, RefundPayment, RestoreInventory, UpdateOrderStatus in sequence'. Dataflow shows PlaceOrder->ValidateItemsAndStock, PlaceOrder->ChargePayment, etc. (sibling orchestration). Sub-child outputs (e.g. ValidateItemsAndStock.validation_result) have consumer=PlaceOrder, not parent. The parent OrderSystem only calls ParseAndValidateInput, PlaceOrder, CancelOrder, TrackOrder directly — it never calls the 8 sub-children. If flattened to parent-mediated, PlaceOrder/CancelOrder become empty orchestrators with no own logic, and the parent must sequence 10+ children for each workflow.
failure_mode: PlaceOrder and CancelOrder explicitly described as calling their sub-children (ValidateItemsAndStock, ChargePayment, etc.). Dataflow shows PlaceOrder->sub-child edges (sibling orchestration). Sub-child outputs consumed by PlaceOrder/CancelOrder, not parent. This is abstraction-level mixing: 2-level hierarchy flattened to 1 level.
